headline_drivers: Q1 2026 earnings beat and raise: AAON reported record Q1 2026 results on 2026-05-07, with revenue and EPS both exceeding consensus and backlog hitting new highs; management simultaneously raised full‑year 2026 guidance (PRNewsWire, Zacks, Seeking Alpha transcript).

Street estimate re‑rating: Following Q1 results, covering analysts materially raised 2026 forecasts from roughly $1.7B to ~$2.0B in revenue (+22% YoY) and EPS from $1.98 to $2.33 (+61%), and increased the average price target ~11% to ~$137 (SimplyWall.St summary referencing five-analyst consensus).

Growth vs. peers narrative: Sell-side and quant screens are highlighting that AAON is now modeled to grow revenue at ~31% CAGR through 2026 vs ~7% for HVAC/industrial peers, framing the story as a structural share‑gainer rather than just a cyclical beneficiary (SimplyWall.St industry comparison).
summary: The recent 40%+ spike in AAON is being driven primarily by a fundamental re‑rating after a very strong Q1 2026 print and a guidance hike on 2026‑05‑07, which confirmed accelerating revenue, expanding margins, and a record backlog. Those results prompted analysts to sharply lift 2026 revenue and EPS estimates (to about $2.0B and $2.33, respectively) and raise price targets by ~11%, reframing AAON as a structurally faster‑growing HVAC/industrial name (forecast ~31% revenue CAGR vs ~7% for peers). With that fundamentals reset in place, the stock is now also benefiting from momentum/technical flows (high RSI, vol‑spike and top‑5d screens), amplifying the move beyond what the earnings surprise alone would typically produce.
